name: Pixel Forge Games – Submissions
description: Indie publisher accepting roguelikes, puzzle, narrative, tactics, survival, arcade. Solo and small teams (up to 15). Playable build or vertical slice required. PC (Steam, Epic, GOG) and/or console (Switch, Xbox). 6–18 month scope preferred.
eligibility_summary: Indie-friendly genres; solo/small team; 6–18 months; PC/console

name: ID@Xbox
description: Microsoft's program for independent developers to publish digital games on Xbox. No fee to apply. Access to dev kits, support, and store presence.
eligibility_summary: Indie devs; digital games for Xbox

name: PlayStation Partners
description: Sony's program for indie and third-party publishers. Support for PS5/PS4 development, funding opportunities, and visibility.

name: Humble Bundle – Publishing
description: Humble publishes indie games and helps with visibility, store pages, and bundles. Focus on PC; interest in diverse and narrative-driven games.
eligibility_summary: Indie PC games; narrative and variety

name: Indie Fund
description: Recoupable investment for indie developers. No equity; you repay from revenue. Looking for strong design and scope that can ship.
eligibility_summary: Indie teams; recoupable funding; no equity

name: Stadia Games and Entertainment Fund (legacy)
description: Historical example: funding for cloud-native and cross-platform indies. Some programs have closed; similar funds exist elsewhere.

name: Epic MegaGrants
description: Epic Games offers grants for projects using Unreal Engine, for open source and media, and for education. Wide scope; no repayment.
eligibility_summary: Unreal projects; games, film, open source, education

name: Nintendo Indie World
description: Nintendo’s visibility channel for indie games on Switch. Submit through your publisher or Nintendo’s partner process for consideration.
eligibility_summary: Switch games; via publisher or dev program
